CalifOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Commission
Novenber 22, L99L
David A. Wi I loughby
2100 Garden Road, Suite A-210
Monterey. CA 93940
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I -91-4 2 9
Dear Mr. wi ll-oughby:
This is in response to your letter requesting assistance on
behalf of the Monterey Peninsula Airport District regarding the
conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the PoIiticaI Reform Act (the
rrtragrr;.1 You have requested infornal assistance.2 Please note
that the Commission's jurisdiction is Iinited to the provisions of
the Act. Other provisions of the 1a$, which may be applicable to
your facts such as Government code section 1090 are outside the
jurisdiction of the cornmission.3
OUESTI ON
what are the responsibilities and potentia). Iiability 
of
members of the Monterey Peninsula Airport District and yourself,
as district 
counsel, where one of the nembers may be violating the
confLict of interest provisions of the Act?
1 co,r..r,..nt Code sections 81oOO-91015. AlL statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 California code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et seq. AII references to regulations are to Titl-e 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
)' 
Regulation 18329(c) (1) provides that the Commissi.on may provide
informaL assistance to any person with a duty to advise other
persons relating to their duties or actions under the Act.
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section
83114; ReguLation 18329(c) (3) . )
' 
t'tothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any
conduct that has already taken pLace. In additj.on, this letter is
based on the facts presented to us. The Commission does not act
as the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re oqelsbv (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 71. )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916)322'5660


Our File No. 
■-9■-429
Page 2
CONCLUS ION
cenerally the Act's requirements pertain to individual
officials, 
not public bodies. Thus, it is the responsibility of
each pubLic official 
to perforn his or her duties in an impartial
manner, free from bias caused by the official's 
own financial
interests or the financial interests of persons srho have supported
the official. 
However, the Act also provides that it is a
viol,ation of the Act for any person to purposely or negligently
cause any other person to violate the Act, or to aid and abet any
other person in the violation of the Act.
rACTS
You are the counsel for the Monterey Peninsula Airport
District (the "districtr'). 
The district 
is governed by a five-
nember board of directors. 
Your duty as district 
counsel is to
advise nembers and ernployees of the dj.strict concerning their
duties under the Act. You stated that the nembers and enployees
of the district 
are subject to the provisions of the Act and
currently file statements of economic interest.
The district 
owns and operates the Monterey Peninsula Airport
and leases property at the airport for office and storage space to
private tenants. In 1985, one building was leased to a business
owned and operated by the brother of a member of the board of
directors of the district 
as an office and for storage needs. The
business subleased a portion of the property to a third party
business for similar uses. The director received no conpensation,
nor or^rned any investnent interest in his brother's business.
The long-term lease was initially 
issued to the nember's
brother. 
However, several years after the lease was entered into,
the lessee was incarcerated and unable to run the business. The
lessee's brother, a director for the district, 
ran the business on
behalf of his brother without compensation. since that time the
Iessee was released, but he continues to be incapacitated. Thus,
the director continues to run the business without compensation.
DI SCUSS ION
You have not asked about the liabi)-ity of the member in
question. Instead, you have asked whether you, or any other
member is liable for the conduct of the nenber in question, j.f the
member in question does violate the Act.
ceneral,ly the Act's requj.rements perta j.n to individual
officiats, 
not public bodies. Thus, it is the responsibility of
each public off j,cj,a1 to perform his or her duties in an impartial
manner, free from bias caused by the official's 
own financial
interests or the financial interests of persons who have supported
the official. 
(Section 81001(b) . )


Our File No. I-9■
-429
Page 3
However, the Act also provides:
Any person who violates any provision of this
title, 
$rho purposely or negligentLy causes any
other person to violate any provision of this
title, 
or who aids and abets any other person in
the violation of any provision of this title, 
shal1
be liable under the provisions of this chapter.
Provided, ho$rever, that this section shaII apply
only to persons lrho have filing or reporting
obligations under this tit1e, or vrho are
compensated for services involving the planning,
organizing, or directing any activity regulated or
required by this titte, 
and that a violation of
this section shall not constitute an additional
violation under chapter 11,
Section 83116. 5.
Thus, a person who has filing or reporting obligations under
the Act may be liable if he neqliqentlv causes another to violate
the Act.{ 
As counsel for the Monterey Peninsula Airport District,
it would be prudent to advise the members of the district 
of their
obligations under the Act and caution them to consult the
Commission regarding potential conflicts of interests. 
Absent
aiding or abetting another person in violatinq the provision of
the title, 
members are not responsible for the conduct of other
nembers.
If you have any further questions regarding this natter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-59oL.'
Sincerely′
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral Counsel
